headline: Health Insurance Coverage Gaps Leave 6 Million Malaysians Exposed
context: An estimated 6 million working-age Malaysians have neither employer health coverage nor private health insurance.

cards.big: 6 million working Malaysians have zero health insurance beyond public hospital access
cards.sub: Gig workers self-employed and informal sector workers are most vulnerable

cards.big: Medical inflation in Malaysia averages 12% annually versus 3% general inflation rate
cards.sub: Single hospitalization can bankrupt an uninsured household earning under RM5,000

cards.big: PeKa B40 health screening program covers basic checks but not treatment costs
cards.sub: Screening without treatment coverage identifies disease without resolving it

cards.big: Taiwan's National Health Insurance covers 99.9% of population for USD800 per capita annually
cards.sub: Single-payer model achieves universal coverage at fraction of US healthcare spending

cards.big: Discovering you have cancer through free screening means nothing if you cannot afford treatment
Should Malaysia implement mandatory national health insurance for all residents
